[Thu 2026-03-19 02:05 EDT] [Previous Feedback] File naming does not meet standards. Should be `20260326_next_week_resource_plan.json` or similar format.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Based on the current sprint progress, please generate a risk assessment report in Markdown format, saved to the day09/ directory. Each risk should include risk_id, description, probability (high/medium/low), impact, and mitigation.

Sprint 8 Progress - Raw Data
===================================

T-402 (API v2 endpoints) - Owner: Chen - Status: in_progress - Progress: 75%, expected done tomorrow
T-403 (Dashboard UI) - Owner: Dev - Status: in_progress - Progress: 60%, blocked since last Monday

T-407 (Email notification service) - Owner: Frank - Status: in_progress - Progress: 40%, started yesterday

- Overall sprint velocity tracking well

arguments.content:
# Sprint 8 Risk Assessment (Mid-point)

Source: `day09/sprint8_progress_raw.txt`

## R-001 — Dashboard UI blocked by API dependency
- **Description:** T-403 (Dashboard UI) has been blocked since last Monday, waiting on T-402 (API v2 endpoints).
- **Probability:** high
- **Impact:** high
- **Mitigation:** Prioritize completion of T-402; align on minimum viable endpoint set; provide stubbed responses/mocks to unblock UI work; run daily sync between Chen and Dev until unblocked.

## R-002 — API v2 schedule slip cascades into UI and integration
- **Description:** T-402 is at 75% and expected done tomorrow. Any delay will cascade into T-403 and downstream integration/QA.
- **Probability:** medium
- **Impact:** high
- **Mitigation:** Lock scope for remaining endpoints; split remaining work into deliverable chunks; publish endpoint contract early (OpenAPI/JSON schema) and support partial handoff.

## R-003 — Late start on OAuth integration
- **Description:** T-405 (OAuth integration) has not started yet and is due next Friday, leaving limited buffer for integration issues.
- **Probability:** medium
- **Impact:** medium
- **Mitigation:** Start discovery/implementation immediately; confirm provider requirements and callback flows; schedule an early integration test window; allocate backup reviewer/pairing support.

## R-004 — Mobile fixes at risk due to Dev availability
- **Description:** T-409 (Mobile responsive fixes) has not started and Dev is the owner. Dev will be on leave Tuesday–Wednesday next week, reducing execution time.
- **Probability:** high
- **Impact:** medium
- **Mitigation:** Reassign or pair another engineer for mobile fixes; break work into small, testable items; prioritize critical viewport issues first.

## R-005 — Email notification service may under-run remaining timeline
- **Description:** T-407 (Email notification service) is at 40% and started yesterday; risk of late discovery of edge cases (deliverability, retries, templates).
- **Probability:** medium
- **Impact:** medium
- **Mitigation:** Define success criteria (retry/backoff, idempotency, bounce handling); add basic observability; start with minimal template set; plan QA coverage early.

## R-006 — Resource coverage gaps next week
- **Description:** Partial coverage constraints next week (Chen out Monday morning; Dev out Tue–Wed; Emma at conference Thu) may slow review/coordination and create bottlenecks.
- **Probability:** medium
- **Impact:** medium
- **Mitigation:** Pre-schedule key reviews and handoffs; front-load dependency resolution (T-402 → T-403); ensure clear owners for on-call decisions during absences.
